title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following the instructions in the letter she believed Bobby wrote, Frankie avoids using her conspicuous Bentley and instead travels via three train transfers to Chipping Sutcliffe. Upon arriving at the Chipping Sutton station in the rain, she follows the directions in the letter to find "Tudor Cottage." When she reaches the cottage, she sees a figure in a tweed suit—whom she identifies as Bobby—peek out of a door before retreating inside. Frankie enters the dark house, detects the scent of chloroform, and is ambushed from behind by an assailant who presses a wet cloth over her mouth, causing her to lose consciousness.

Frankie wakes up tied to a hard floor in a dark attic containing an old iron bed, broken chairs, and a coal box. She discovers that Bobby is also tied up in the corner with a gag. After Frankie helps him remove the gag, they realize they have been tricked. Bobby reveals that he never wrote the letter Frankie followed and explains that he was drugged via food after arriving from Granchester. Because Frankie kept the letter a secret from Roger, they realize no one knows their location. As they attempt to free themselves, Dr. Nicholson enters the attic. He mocks them for being easily deceived and examines their bindings, appearing satisfied with Bobby's restraints but expressing dissatisfaction with Frankie's.
